message:
fix(apr): use atomic file creation for fallback lock acquisition

Improves the fallback file-based locking mechanism in acquire_lock()
to prevent race conditions:

Before:
- Check if lock file exists
- If exists, read PID and check if running
- If not running or file doesn't exist, write PID

Problem: Time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) race between checking
for existence and writing. Two processes could both see no lock file
and both attempt to acquire.

After:
- Try atomic creation with noclobber (set -C; echo $$ > file)
- If that fails, check if lock is stale (process not running)
- If stale, remove and retry atomic creation once
- Fail if atomic creation fails on retry

The noclobber shell option (-C) causes the redirection to fail if the
file already exists, making the create-and-write atomic from the
shell's perspective. This eliminates the TOCTOU window.

Note: This only affects the fallback path when flock is unavailable.
The primary path using flock with file descriptors is already atomic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-hqt","title":"Set up BATS testing framework and directory structure","description":"# Task: Set up BATS Testing Framework

## Objective
Install and configure BATS (Bash Automated Testing System) for APR unit and integration testing.

## Deliverables

### 1. Directory Structure
```
tests/
├── unit/                    # Unit tests for individual functions
│   ├── test_utils.bats      # Utility function tests
│   ├── test_config.bats     # Config parsing tests
│   ├── test_output.bats     # Output function tests
│   ├── test_stream.bats     # Stream separation tests (NEW)
│   └── test_lock.bats       # Lock mechanism tests
├── integration/             # Command-level tests
│   ├── test_setup.bats      # Setup wizard tests
│   ├── test_run.bats        # Run command tests
│   ├── test_commands.bats   # Other commands
│   ├── test_robot.bats      # Robot mode tests
│   └── test_install.bats    # Install script tests (NEW)
├── e2e/                     # End-to-end workflow tests
│   ├── test_full_workflow.bats
│   └── test_error_recovery.bats
├── fixtures/                # Test data
│   ├── configs/             # Sample workflow configs
│   ├── documents/           # Sample README, spec files
│   └── outputs/             # Expected outputs
├── helpers/                 # Test utilities
│   ├── test_helper.bash     # Common setup/teardown
│   ├── logging.bash         # Detailed test logging
│   └── assertions.bash      # Custom assertions
├── run_tests.sh             # Test runner with logging
└── ci_runner.sh             # CI-specific runner (NEW)
```

### 2. BATS Installation
- Add bats-core as submodule or document installation
- Include bats-support and bats-assert helpers
- Verify Bash 4.0+ compatibility
- Document BATS version requirements

### 3. Test Helper Functions
```bash
# test_helper.bash should include:
- setup_test_environment()    # Create isolated temp dir
- teardown_test_environment() # Cleanup
- load_apr_functions()        # Source apr for unit testing
- log_test_step()            # Detailed logging
- assert_file_contains()     # Custom assertions
- assert_exit_code()         # Exit code verification
- assert_stderr_only()       # Stream separation (NEW)
- assert_stdout_only()       # Stream separation (NEW)
- assert_valid_json()        # JSON validation (NEW)
- setup_mock_xdg()           # XDG path testing (NEW)
```

### 4. Logging Infrastructure
- Each test logs: test name, inputs, expected output, actual output
- Log file per test run with timestamp
- Summary report at end
- JUnit XML output for CI integration (NEW)

### 5. CI Integration
- GitHub Actions workflow for running tests
- ShellCheck for test files themselves
- Test matrix: Ubuntu, macOS, Bash 4.x/5.x

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] BATS framework installed and working
- [ ] Directory structure created
- [ ] test_helper.bash with setup/teardown
- [ ] logging.bash with detailed logging
- [ ] run_tests.sh executes all tests
- [ ] ci_runner.sh for CI environments
- [ ] Sample test passes
- [ ] ShellCheck passes on test files","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:07:33.597862458-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T21:24:10.819018789-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T21:24:10.819018789-05:00","close_reason":"Testing infrastructure complete: 180 tests (78 unit, 50 integration, 19 E2E)"}
 {"id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-ifg","title":"Unit Tests: Output Functions (print_*, spin, confirm, choose, input)","description":"# Task: Unit Tests for Output Functions

## Objective
Test all terminal output functions with gum, ANSI fallback, NO_COLOR, and stream separation.

## Functions to Test

### 1. print_banner() - Main Banner Display
```bash
# Test cases:
- GUM_AVAILABLE=true → gum output
- GUM_AVAILABLE=false → ANSI output
- QUIET_MODE=true → no output
- Terminal width adaptation (60 max, 40 min)
- Version number displayed correctly
```

### 2. print_* Functions (success, error, warning, info, dim, header)
```bash
# For each function test:
- Output goes to stderr (CRITICAL - verify stream separation)
- GUM_AVAILABLE=true → gum styling
- GUM_AVAILABLE=false → ANSI codes
- QUIET_MODE suppresses appropriate functions
- Correct emoji/prefix (✓, ✗, ⚠, ℹ)

# Note: print_error should NOT be suppressed by QUIET_MODE
```

### 3. print_step() - Step Progress Display
```bash
# Test cases:
- Normal step: [1/5] message
- Optional step: [Optional] message
- GUM vs ANSI modes
- QUIET_MODE suppression
```

### 4. spin() - Spinner Wrapper
```bash
# Test cases:
- GUM_AVAILABLE=true → gum spin
- GUM_AVAILABLE=false → simple message
- QUIET_MODE → no visual, command still runs
- Command exit code preserved
```

### 5. confirm() - User Confirmation
```bash
# Test cases:
- GUM_AVAILABLE=true → gum confirm
- ANSI fallback with [Y/n] or [y/N] prompts
- Default true/false behavior
- Non-interactive mode returns default
- Case insensitivity (Y/y/YES/yes)
```

### 6. choose() - Selection Menu
```bash
# Test cases:
- GUM_AVAILABLE=true → gum choose
- ANSI fallback with numbered list
- Default to first option on invalid input
- Output goes to stdout (selection result)
```

### 7. input() - Text Input
```bash
# Test cases:
- GUM_AVAILABLE=true → gum input
- ANSI fallback with read
- Default value handling
- Empty input handling
```

### 8. file_picker() - File Selection
```bash
# Test cases:
- GUM_AVAILABLE=true → gum file (no --all flag)
- ANSI fallback prompts for path
- Hidden files excluded in gum mode
```

## NEW: Environment Variable Testing

### 9. NO_COLOR Support (v1.1.0 feature)
```bash
# Test cases:
- NO_COLOR=1 → no ANSI escape codes in output
- NO_COLOR= (empty) → colors enabled
- NO_COLOR unset → colors enabled
- Verify regex ^\\x1b does NOT match when NO_COLOR=1
- Test all print_* functions respect NO_COLOR
```

### 10. APR_NO_GUM Support
```bash
# Test cases:
- APR_NO_GUM=1 → gum not used even if available
- APR_NO_GUM= (empty) → gum used if available
- GUM_AVAILABLE set correctly based on APR_NO_GUM
```

### 11. CI Environment Detection
```bash
# Test cases:
- CI=true → gum suppressed
- GITHUB_ACTIONS=true → gum suppressed
- Interactive prompts return defaults in CI
```

## NEW: Stream Separation Tests
```bash
# CRITICAL: AGENTS.md requires stderr for human output, stdout for structured data

# Test cases:
- print_success outputs ONLY to stderr
- print_error outputs ONLY to stderr
- print_info outputs ONLY to stderr
- Robot mode JSON outputs ONLY to stdout
- No stderr pollution in robot mode output
- Capture both streams separately and verify

# Implementation:
test_stream_separation() {
    # Capture stderr and stdout separately
    { stdout=$( { stderr=$(print_success "test"); } 2>&1; echo "$stderr"); } 
    
    # Verify stdout is empty, stderr has content
    assert_equal "$stdout" ""
    assert [ -n "$stderr" ]
}
```

## Testing Approach
- Capture stderr and stdout SEPARATELY for verification
- Use NO_COLOR=1 for deterministic ANSI output testing
- Test all GUM_AVAILABLE=true/false paths
- Test all environment variable combinations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All print_* functions tested in both gum/ANSI modes
- [ ] Interactive functions tested with simulated input
- [ ] QUIET_MODE behavior verified for each function
- [ ] stderr vs stdout verified for EVERY function
- [ ] NO_COLOR=1 disables all ANSI codes
- [ ] APR_NO_GUM=1 forces ANSI fallback
- [ ] CI environment detection works
- [ ] Detailed logs showing actual vs expected output","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:08:20.763981354-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T21:00:13.055905321-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T21:00:13.055905321-05:00","close_reason":"Unit tests implemented in test_output.bats - 24 tests covering print_* functions, stderr output, NO_COLOR, QUIET_MODE behavior. Interactive functions (spin, confirm, choose, input) deferred as they require TTY","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-ifg","depends_on_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-hqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:12:37.298421653-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-it2","title":"Integration Tests: Robot Mode Commands (JSON API)","description":"# Task: Integration Tests for Robot Mode

## Objective
Test the complete robot mode JSON API for coding agent integration.

## Command Tests

### 1. apr robot status
```bash
# Not configured:
run apr robot status
# Verify:
- Valid JSON
- configured: false
- Hint about initialization

# Configured:
# Setup: Initialize .apr
run apr robot status
# Verify:
- configured: true
- default_workflow set
- workflows array populated
- oracle_available status correct
```

### 2. apr robot workflows
```bash
# No workflows:
run apr robot workflows
# Verify: not_configured error

# With workflows:
run apr robot workflows
# Verify:
- ok: true
- workflows array with name/description
- Valid JSON
```

### 3. apr robot init
```bash
# Fresh init:
run apr robot init
# Verify:
- ok: true
- created: true
- .apr directory created

# Already initialized:
run apr robot init
# Verify:
- ok: true
- created: false
- existed: true
```

### 4. apr robot validate <round>
```bash
# Valid state:
# Setup: Complete workflow with files
run apr robot validate 1
# Verify:
- ok: true
- valid: true
- empty errors array

# Invalid state:
# Setup: Missing required files
run apr robot validate 1
# Verify:
- ok: false
- valid: false
- errors array populated
```

### 5. apr robot run <round>
```bash
# Note: Actually starts Oracle in background
# Test validation only (don't wait for Oracle):

# Missing round:
run apr robot run
# Verify: missing_argument error

# Invalid round:
run apr robot run abc
# Verify: invalid_argument error

# Workflow options:
run apr robot run 1 -w myworkflow
# Verify: Correct workflow used

# Include impl:
run apr robot run 1 --include-impl
# Verify: include_impl in response
```

### 6. apr robot history
```bash
# No rounds:
run apr robot history
# Verify: not_found error

# With rounds:
run apr robot history
# Verify:
- ok: true
- count correct
- rounds array with round, file, size, modified
- All numbers are JSON numbers (not strings)
```

### 7. apr robot help
```bash
run apr robot help
# Verify:
- Valid JSON
- All commands documented
- Examples included
```

### 8. Robot Mode Options
```bash
# Compact output:
run apr robot status --compact
# Verify: Minified JSON (no newlines/indentation)

# Workflow selection:
run apr robot history -w myworkflow
# Verify: Correct workflow used

# Error output:
run apr robot unknown_command
# Verify: unknown_command error, valid JSON
```

### 9. jq Requirement
```bash
# Test without jq (mock unavailable):
# Verify: Appropriate error message in JSON-like format
```

## JSON Validation
```bash
# All tests should verify:
validate_robot_json() {
    local output="$1"
    
    # Must be valid JSON
    echo "$output" | jq . > /dev/null || fail "Invalid JSON"
    
    # Must have envelope structure
    echo "$output" | jq -e '.ok != null' > /dev/null || fail "Missing .ok"
    echo "$output" | jq -e '.code != null' > /dev/null || fail "Missing .code"
    echo "$output" | jq -e '.data != null' > /dev/null || fail "Missing .data"
    echo "$output" | jq -e '.meta.v != null' > /dev/null || fail "Missing .meta.v"
    echo "$output" | jq -e '.meta.ts != null' > /dev/null || fail "Missing .meta.ts"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All robot commands produce valid JSON
- [ ] Error codes meaningful and documented
- [ ] Hints helpful for error resolution
- [ ] --compact mode works
- [ ] -w workflow selection works everywhere
- [ ] No stderr pollution of JSON output
- [ ] jq can parse all outputs
- [ ] Coding agents can parse and use output","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:10:54.188078289-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T20:10:54.188078289-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-it2","depends_on_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-de5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:16:50.329266777-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-it2","depends_on_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-uos","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:16:50.356743698-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-iw3","title":"Integration Tests: Management Commands (list, history, show, status, attach)","description":"# Task: Integration Tests for Management Commands

## Objective
Test all workflow management commands with real data.

## Commands to Test

### 1. apr list - List Workflows
```bash
# No workflows:
apr list
# Verify: "No workflows configured yet" message

# With workflows:
# Setup: Create multiple workflows
apr list
# Verify:
- All workflows listed
- Descriptions shown
- Default workflow marked
- Both gum and ANSI output tested
```

### 2. apr history - Revision History
```bash
# No rounds:
apr history
# Verify: "No rounds recorded yet" message

# With rounds:
# Setup: Create some round output files
apr history
# Verify:
- All rounds listed
- File sizes shown
- Dates shown (cross-platform)
- Latest round marked
- Preview of first line shown

# Workflow selection:
apr history -w myworkflow
# Verify: Shows history for specific workflow
```

### 3. apr show <round> - View Round Output
```bash
# Round exists:
apr show 1
# Verify: Content displayed (bat/less/cat fallback)

# Round doesn't exist:
apr show 99
# Verify: Error message with helpful hint

# Workflow selection:
apr show 1 -w myworkflow
# Verify: Shows correct workflow's round
```

### 4. apr status - Oracle Session Status
```bash
# Note: This calls Oracle, so may need mock or skip in CI

# Test command execution:
apr status
# Verify: Oracle status command executed

# Test --hours option:
apr status --hours 24
# Verify: Correct hours passed to Oracle
```

### 5. apr attach <session> - Attach to Session
```bash
# Note: This calls Oracle, so may need mock or skip in CI

# Test command execution:
apr attach apr-default-round-1
# Verify: Oracle session command executed with --render
```

### 6. apr diff <N> [M] - Compare Rounds
```bash
# Setup: Create round files with different content

# Single round (compare with previous):
apr diff 3
# Verify: Compares round 3 with round 2

# Two rounds:
apr diff 2 5
# Verify: Compares round 2 with round 5

# Round 1 alone:
apr diff 1
# Verify: Error (no previous round)

# Missing round:
apr diff 99
# Verify: Error message

# Tool detection:
# With delta: Uses delta
# Without delta: Falls back to diff
```

### 7. apr integrate <round> - Generate Integration Prompt
```bash
# Test basic:
apr integrate 3
# Verify: Integration prompt generated to stdout

# Test --copy:
apr integrate 3 --copy
# Verify: Copied to clipboard (if available)

# Test --output:
apr integrate 3 --output /tmp/prompt.md
# Verify: Written to file
```

### 8. apr stats - Round Analytics
```bash
# No rounds:
apr stats
# Verify: Appropriate message

# With rounds:
apr stats
# Verify:
- Round count
- Average size
- Trend signal (if enough rounds)
- Table of rounds with sizes/dates
```

## Test Fixtures
```
tests/fixtures/rounds/
├── round_1.md    # Small round output
├── round_2.md    # Medium round output
├── round_3.md    # Large round output
└── round_4.md    # Different content for diff testing
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All management commands tested
- [ ] Empty state handling correct
- [ ] Workflow selection working
- [ ] Error messages helpful
- [ ] Both gum and ANSI modes tested
- [ ] Cross-platform compatibility verified","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:10:33.908224761-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T21:43:55.970086731-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-iw3","depends_on_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-uos","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:16:50.270620839-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-iw3","depends_on_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-ifg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:16:50.300379198-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-ixw","title":"Unit Tests: Lock Mechanism (acquire_lock, release_lock, cleanup_temp)","description":"# Task: Unit Tests for Lock and Cleanup Mechanisms

## Objective
Test concurrent execution prevention and cleanup mechanisms with REAL locking behavior.

## Functions to Test

### 1. acquire_lock() - Lock Acquisition
```bash
# Test cases with flock available:
- First process acquires lock → returns 0
- Second process fails to acquire → returns 1
- Lock file created in correct location
- PID written to lock file

# Test cases with flock fallback:
- APR_LOCK_FD not used (file-based only)
- Stale lock detection (PID not running)
- Stale lock cleanup before acquisition

# Concurrent test:
- Start background process holding lock
- Attempt to acquire from foreground
- Verify failure
- Kill background, verify reacquisition works

# Edge cases:
- Lock directory doesn't exist → created
- Lock file permissions
- Workflow/round naming in lock file path
```

### 2. release_lock() - Lock Release
```bash
# Test cases:
- Release after flock acquisition → FD closed
- Release after file-based lock → file deleted
- Release without acquisition → no error
- APR_LOCK_FILE and APR_LOCK_FD cleared
- Idempotent (safe to call multiple times)
```

### 3. cleanup_temp() - Exit Cleanup
```bash
# Test cases:
- APR_TEMP_DIR set and exists → removed
- APR_TEMP_DIR empty → no error
- APR_TEMP_DIR doesn't exist → no error
- Lock released during cleanup
- Trap triggers on EXIT
- Trap triggers on INT (Ctrl+C simulation)
- Trap triggers on TERM

# Real cleanup test:
- Create temp dir
- Set APR_TEMP_DIR
- Exit script
- Verify temp dir removed
```

### 4. Trap Integration
```bash
# Test the trap registration:
- trap cleanup_temp EXIT INT TERM
- Verify cleanup runs on normal exit
- Verify cleanup runs on error exit (set -e trigger)
- Verify cleanup runs on signal
```

## Testing Approach
```bash
# Example: Concurrent lock test
test_concurrent_lock() {
    local lock_dir=$(mktemp -d)
    export CONFIG_DIR="$lock_dir"
    
    # Background process holds lock
    (
        source apr
        acquire_lock "test" "1"
        sleep 10
    ) &
    local bg_pid=$!
    sleep 0.5  # Let it acquire
    
    # Foreground should fail
    source apr
    if acquire_lock "test" "1"; then
        fail "Should not acquire lock"
    fi
    
    # Cleanup
    kill $bg_pid 2>/dev/null
    wait $bg_pid 2>/dev/null
    rm -rf "$lock_dir"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] acquire_lock tested with flock and fallback
- [ ] Concurrent lock prevention verified
- [ ] Stale lock detection works
- [ ] release_lock properly cleans up
- [ ] cleanup_temp removes temp directories
- [ ] Trap fires on all exit conditions

 {"id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-k9q","title":"Integration Tests: install.sh Installer Script","description":"# Task: Integration Tests for install.sh

## Objective
Test the curl-bash installer script comprehensively.

## CRITICAL: This is a security-sensitive component
The installer downloads and executes code. Tests must verify security measures.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Fresh Installation
```bash
# Test clean install to default location:
export APR_INSTALL_DIR="$TEST_DIR/bin"
mkdir -p "$APR_INSTALL_DIR"

./install.sh

# Verify:
- [ ] Script downloaded to correct location
- [ ] Script is executable (chmod +x)
- [ ] Script runs (apr --version works)
- [ ] Installation message shown
```

### 2. Custom Installation Directory
```bash
# Test APR_INSTALL_DIR override:
export APR_INSTALL_DIR="/tmp/custom_bin"

./install.sh

# Verify:
- [ ] Installed to custom directory
- [ ] PATH warning if not in PATH
```

### 3. Update Existing Installation
```bash
# Test reinstall over existing:
# Pre-install v1.0.0
# Run installer for v1.1.0

./install.sh

# Verify:
- [ ] Old version replaced
- [ ] Backup created (if applicable)
- [ ] Version updated
```

### 4. Permission Handling
```bash
# Test when install dir not writable:
chmod 555 "$APR_INSTALL_DIR"

./install.sh

# Verify:
- [ ] Helpful error message
- [ ] Suggests sudo or alternative
- [ ] Non-zero exit code
```

### 5. Download Verification
```bash
# Test shebang check:
# Verify installer checks downloaded script is valid bash

# Test checksum verification (v1.1.0):
# Verify SHA256 checksum is validated when available

# Test download failure:
# Mock network failure, verify graceful handling
```

### 6. Environment Detection
```bash
# Test curl vs wget:
# When curl available → uses curl
# When only wget available → uses wget
# When neither → helpful error

# Test destination detection:
# /usr/local/bin if writable
# ~/bin if exists
# ~/.local/bin as fallback
```

### 7. Version Selection
```bash
# Test latest version (default):
./install.sh
# Verify: Gets latest from releases

# Test specific version (if supported):
APR_VERSION=1.0.0 ./install.sh
# Verify: Gets specific version
```

### 8. Network Error Handling
```bash
# Test timeout:
# Mock slow server, verify timeout handling

# Test 404:
# Mock missing file, verify error message

# Test redirect:
# Verify follows redirects correctly
```

### 9. CI Environment
```bash
# Test non-interactive install:
CI=true ./install.sh

# Verify:
- [ ] No prompts
- [ ] Sensible defaults
- [ ] Clear output
```

### 10. Cleanup on Failure
```bash
# Test partial download cleanup:
# Interrupt download, verify no partial files left

# Verify temp directory cleaned
```

## Test Implementation
```bash
@test "install.sh installs to APR_INSTALL_DIR" {
    export APR_INSTALL_DIR="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/bin"
    mkdir -p "$APR_INSTALL_DIR"
    
    run ./install.sh
    
    log_test_output "$output"
    
    assert_success
    assert [ -x "$APR_INSTALL_DIR/apr" ]
    
    # Verify its valid
    run "$APR_INSTALL_DIR/apr" --version
    assert_success
}

@test "install.sh shows error for non-writable directory" {
    export APR_INSTALL_DIR="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/readonly"
    mkdir -p "$APR_INSTALL_DIR"
    chmod 555 "$APR_INSTALL_DIR"
    
    run ./install.sh
    
    assert_failure
    assert_output --partial "permission"
}
```

## Security Verification
```bash
# CRITICAL security tests:

- [ ] Downloaded script has correct shebang
- [ ] Checksum matches when provided
- [ ] No execution of downloaded content before validation
- [ ] No shell injection vulnerabilities in URL handling
- [ ] HTTPS used for all downloads
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fresh install works
- [ ] Custom directory works
- [ ] Update/reinstall works
- [ ] Permission errors handled gracefully
- [ ] Download verification (shebang, checksum)
- [ ] Both curl and wget tested
- [ ] CI mode works
- [ ] All failure modes have helpful messages

 {"id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-ufc","title":"Testing Infrastructure: Complete test coverage for APR","description":"# APR Testing Infrastructure Epic

## Overview
Implement comprehensive testing for APR including unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end tests. All tests should use REAL behavior (no mocks/fakes) and include detailed logging.

## Philosophy
- **No mocks**: Test actual functions with real inputs/outputs
- **Isolated environments**: Use temp directories for each test
- **Detailed logging**: Every test logs what it's doing for debugging
- **Deterministic**: Tests should be reproducible

## Test Categories
1. **Unit Tests**: Individual function testing with BATS
2. **Integration Tests**: Command-level testing with real configs
3. **E2E Tests**: Full workflow testing with logging

## Success Criteria
- 100% function coverage for utility functions
- All commands have integration tests
- Full workflow E2E tests pass
- CI integration working","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:07:17.440688231-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T20:07:17.440688231-05:00"}

+# Robot: Show - View round content with metadata
+robot_show() {
+    local round_num="${1:-}"
+    local workflow="${WORKFLOW:-default}"
+
+    if [[ -z "$round_num" ]]; then
+        robot_json false "missing_argument" '{"argument":"round"}' "Round number required"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    # Verify workflow exists
+    local workflow_file="$CONFIG_DIR/workflows/${workflow}.yaml"
+    if [[ ! -f "$workflow_file" ]]; then
+        robot_json false "not_found" "$(jq -nc --arg wf "$workflow" '{resource: "workflow", name: $wf}')" "Workflow not found"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    local round_file="$CONFIG_DIR/rounds/$workflow/round_${round_num}.md"
+    if [[ ! -f "$round_file" ]]; then
+        robot_json false "not_found" "$(jq -nc --arg r "$round_num" --arg wf "$workflow" '{resource: "round", round: $r, workflow: $wf}')" "Round not found"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    local content char_count line_count word_count mtime
+    content=$(cat "$round_file")
+    char_count=${#content}
+    line_count=$(wc -l < "$round_file" | tr -d ' ')
+    word_count=$(wc -w < "$round_file" | tr -d ' ')
+    mtime=$(stat -c %Y "$round_file" 2>/dev/null || stat -f %m "$round_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
+
+    local data
+    data=$(jq -nc \
+        --arg workflow "$workflow" \
+        --argjson round "$round_num" \
+        --arg content "$content" \
+        --argjson char_count "$char_count" \
+        --argjson line_count "$line_count" \
+        --argjson word_count "$word_count" \
+        --argjson mtime "$mtime" \
+        --arg path "$round_file" \
+        '{
+            workflow: $workflow,
+            round: $round,
+            content: $content,
+            stats: {
+                chars: $char_count,
+                lines: $line_count,
+                words: $word_count
+            },
+            mtime: $mtime,
+            path: $path
+        }')
+
+    robot_json true "ok" "$data"
+}
+
+# Robot: Diff - Compare two rounds
+robot_diff() {
+    local round_a="${1:-}"
+    local round_b="${2:-}"
+    local workflow="${WORKFLOW:-default}"
+
+    if [[ -z "$round_a" ]]; then
+        robot_json false "missing_argument" '{"argument":"round_a"}' "At least one round number required"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    # Verify workflow exists
+    local workflow_file="$CONFIG_DIR/workflows/${workflow}.yaml"
+    if [[ ! -f "$workflow_file" ]]; then
+        robot_json false "not_found" "$(jq -nc --arg wf "$workflow" '{resource: "workflow", name: $wf}')" "Workflow not found"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    local rounds_dir="$CONFIG_DIR/rounds/$workflow"
+
+    # If only one round given, compare with previous
+    if [[ -z "$round_b" ]]; then
+        if [[ "$round_a" -le 1 ]]; then
+            robot_json false "invalid_argument" '{"argument":"round_a","reason":"no_previous"}' "Round 1 has no previous round to compare with"
+            return 1
+        fi
+        round_b=$((round_a - 1))
+    fi
+
+    local file_a="$rounds_dir/round_${round_a}.md"
+    local file_b="$rounds_dir/round_${round_b}.md"
+
+    if [[ ! -f "$file_a" ]]; then
+        robot_json false "not_found" "$(jq -nc --arg r "$round_a" --arg wf "$workflow" '{resource: "round", round: $r, workflow: $wf}')" "Round $round_a not found"
+        return 1
+    fi
+    if [[ ! -f "$file_b" ]]; then
+        robot_json false "not_found" "$(jq -nc --arg r "$round_b" --arg wf "$workflow" '{resource: "round", round: $r, workflow: $wf}')" "Round $round_b not found"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    # Get file stats
+    local size_a size_b lines_a lines_b
+    size_a=$(stat -c %s "$file_a" 2>/dev/null || stat -f %z "$file_a" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
+    size_b=$(stat -c %s "$file_b" 2>/dev/null || stat -f %z "$file_b" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
+    lines_a=$(wc -l < "$file_a" | tr -d ' ')
+    lines_b=$(wc -l < "$file_b" | tr -d ' ')
+
+    # Generate unified diff
+    local diff_output diff_stats
+    diff_output=$(diff -u "$file_b" "$file_a" 2>/dev/null || true)
+
+    # Calculate diff stats
+    local additions deletions
+    additions=$(echo "$diff_output" | grep -c '^+[^+]' || echo "0")
+    deletions=$(echo "$diff_output" | grep -c '^-[^-]' || echo "0")
+
+    local data
+    data=$(jq -nc \
+        --arg workflow "$workflow" \
+        --argjson round_a "$round_a" \
+        --argjson round_b "$round_b" \
+        --argjson size_a "$size_a" \
+        --argjson size_b "$size_b" \
+        --argjson lines_a "$lines_a" \
+        --argjson lines_b "$lines_b" \
+        --argjson additions "$additions" \
+        --argjson deletions "$deletions" \
+        --arg diff "$diff_output" \
+        '{
+            workflow: $workflow,
+            comparing: {from: $round_b, to: $round_a},
+            stats: {
+                before: {bytes: $size_b, lines: $lines_b},
+                after: {bytes: $size_a, lines: $lines_a},
+                changes: {additions: $additions, deletions: $deletions}
+            },
+            diff: $diff
+        }')
+
+    robot_json true "ok" "$data"
+}
+
+# Robot: Integrate - Generate Claude Code integration prompt
+robot_integrate() {
+    local round_num="${1:-}"
+    local workflow="${WORKFLOW:-default}"
+
+    if [[ -z "$round_num" ]]; then
+        robot_json false "missing_argument" '{"argument":"round"}' "Round number required"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    # Verify workflow exists
+    local workflow_file="$CONFIG_DIR/workflows/${workflow}.yaml"
+    if [[ ! -f "$workflow_file" ]]; then
+        robot_json false "not_found" "$(jq -nc --arg wf "$workflow" '{resource: "workflow", name: $wf}')" "Workflow not found"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    # Get spec path
+    local spec_path
+    spec_path=$(yaml_unquote "$(get_config_value "spec" "$workflow_file")")
+    if [[ -z "$spec_path" ]]; then
+        robot_json false "config_error" '{"field":"spec"}' "Spec path not configured in workflow"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    local round_file="$CONFIG_DIR/rounds/$workflow/round_${round_num}.md"
+    if [[ ! -f "$round_file" ]]; then
+        robot_json false "not_found" "$(jq -nc --arg r "$round_num" --arg wf "$workflow" '{resource: "round", round: $r, workflow: $wf}')" "Round not found"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    local round_content round_size
+    round_content=$(cat "$round_file")
+    round_size=${#round_content}
+
+    # Build the integration prompt
+    local prompt
+    prompt=$(cat <<'PROMPT_HEADER'
+First read ALL of the AGENTS.md file and README.md file super carefully
+and understand ALL of both! Then use your code investigation agent mode
+to fully understand the code and technical architecture of the project.
+PROMPT_HEADER
+)
+    prompt+="
+Read ALL of the specification document at: $spec_path
+
+Now integrate the following feedback from GPT Pro 5.2 Extended Reasoning
+(Round $round_num of the APR iterative refinement process). For each
+suggestion, carefully evaluate whether you agree with the proposed change.
+Then apply the changes you endorse to the specification document.
+
+Be meticulous and use ultrathink.
+
+---
+
+$round_content"
+
+    local data
+    data=$(jq -nc \
+        --arg workflow "$workflow" \
+        --argjson round "$round_num" \
+        --arg spec_path "$spec_path" \
+        --arg prompt "$prompt" \
+        --argjson round_size "$round_size" \
+        --argjson prompt_size "${#prompt}" \
+        '{
+            workflow: $workflow,
+            round: $round,
+            spec_path: $spec_path,
+            prompt: $prompt,
+            stats: {
+                round_chars: $round_size,
+                prompt_chars: $prompt_size
+            }
+        }')
+
+    robot_json true "ok" "$data" "Copy .data.prompt to Claude Code"
+}
+
